title: ranunculus trichophyllus proles capillifolius
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus proles capillifolius, also known as creeping buttercup, is a prostrate perennial herb of the ranunculus genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and creeping stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and calm muscle spasms. The health benefits of Ranunculus trichophyllus proles capillifolius include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them in tinctures and salves, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
